behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is usually a contract drift between the live embedding model output and index dimension. A stubbed embedding response can be made to fit and hide this. This behavior depends on real AI/ML output (embedding shape), so Integration is too low; Workflow is not required because this behavior is only about the embedding-to-index contract, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with representative queries, then assert returned vector length exactly matches the configured vector index dimension used by the search service; fail if any mismatch is observed.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff correctness between search service and vector DB query/sort behavior. It does not require real AI/ML judgment, and no OS/network/subprocess is required if using a local/in-process test index. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is specifically about ordering by cosine score, not LLM relevance decisions or full pipeline orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test vector index with fixed vectors and expected cosine relationships, run similarity search through the service layer, and assert scores are monotonically non-increasing in returned order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment for nuanced intent; mocks would always pass if pre-scripted and hide failures in model behavior or prompt steering. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model decision point ('the LLM re-ranking step'), not a multi-step agent pipeline assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real re-ranker on curated nuanced queries where lexical similarity conflicts with contextual intent, compare raw similarity order vs re-ranked order, and assert at least one position changes on a required share of cases.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Whether a query is truly 'no close match' is semantic and depends on embedding-model output behavior against real corpus vectors. Deterministic stubs can hide this by manufacturing clean separations. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is about a single decision outcome (empty vs noisy results), not correctness across multiple agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real embedding model and realistic corpus index, run adversarial out-of-domain queries expected to have no semantic neighbors; assert API returns empty results (or below minimum relevance threshold) instead of arbitrary low-score items.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is full-pipeline SLO validation across multiple agentic steps in sequence (embedding + LLM rerank) plus search and API handling. Mocking model calls would hide true latency. Rejected lower levels: behavior text explicitly says 'end-to-end latency ... to returning re-ranked results,' which requires the complete chained path, not isolated module timing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end API load for median-case queries with real embedding and re-ranking calls enabled, measure p50 latency over sufficient sample size, and assert median response time is under 2.0s.
